title: GCD thread-pool exhaustion via connection flood (pre-auth)
description: acceptThread() spawned a DispatchQueue.global.async block for every accepted socket without any limit. Opening 50+ simultaneous connections before authentication saturated the GCD default thread pool, making the server unresponsive. The pending connections were never counted — only authenticated clients in connectedClients were tracked.
exploit: Open 50+ TCP connections to port 4871 without completing the P7 handshake.
patch_description: Added pendingConnectionCount atomic counter (protected by NSLock) in ServerController. Before spawning the async block, acceptThread() checks pendingConnections + connectedClients.count >= maxConcurrentConnections (100). If over limit, the raw socket is closed immediately without spawning a thread. The counter is incremented before the block and decremented in a defer block regardless of outcome.
